scenario.name: T1-Functional
scenario.description: Direct stack-processor invocation with synthetic CloudTrail event for drift-test-01. Verifies LLM call, validator, and PR creation end-to-end.
scenario.objective: Verify the complete drift detection pipeline for a single stack without CloudTrail ingestion overhead
scenario.expected_outcome: stack-processor invokes Bedrock (latency_ms logged), validator accepts template, pr-creator opens a GitHub PR

scenario.name: T2-Scaling
scenario.description: 10 stacks drifted (round 1), orchestrator processes all in parallel. Measures Lambda horizontal scale-out and throughput at moderate load.
scenario.objective: Verify Lambda concurrency scales to handle 10 parallel stacks and measure throughput baseline
scenario.expected_outcome: 10 parallel stack-processor invocations, peak ConcurrentExecutions = 10, no throttles

scenario.name: T3-Performance
scenario.description: 10 stacks drifted across all 3 rounds (increasing complexity), then processed together. Highest event density per stack — primary P95/P99 benchmark scenario.
scenario.objective: Measure P95/P99 Bedrock latency and peak throughput under maximum drift event density
scenario.expected_outcome: Bedrock P99 < 120000 ms, throughput >= T2, no Lambda crashes or DLQ messages from valid tenants

scenario.name: T4-Failure
scenario.description: Bad tenant with non-existent IAM role injected into DynamoDB. Orchestrator logs ERROR for that tenant and continues processing valid tenants. DLQ should remain empty (no Lambda crashes).
scenario.objective: Verify graceful per-tenant error isolation: one bad tenant must not block or crash processing of valid tenants
scenario.expected_outcome: ERROR log for 'load-test-bad-tenant', valid tenant stacks processed normally, DLQ depth = 0, Lambda invocation count unchanged
scenario.fault: Invalid role_arn in DynamoDB tenant record causes STS AssumeRole ClientError

scenario.name: T5-Security
scenario.description: CloudWatch log audit for credential leakage. Searches for AWS access key patterns (AKIA), GitHub tokens (ghp_), and password keywords. Also verifies cross-account role assumption is logged correctly.
scenario.objective: Confirm no secrets are logged to CloudWatch; verify IAM cross-account flow is auditable
scenario.expected_outcome: Zero occurrences of AKIA / ghp_ / password in logs; role ARN visible in processor logs
scenario.security_scan_result: [STEP] Scanning CloudWatch logs for credential leakage...  [INFO]   Pattern 'AKIA': NOT FOUND in logs (expected)  [INFO]   Pattern 'ghp_': NOT FOUND in logs (expected)  [INFO]   Pattern 'password': NOT FOUND in logs (expected)  [INFO]   Pattern 'secret_key': NOT FOUND in logs (expected)  [INFO]   Pattern 'aws_secret': NOT FOUND in logs (expected)  [WARN]   Cross-account role assumption not found in processor logs — check tenant config  [INFO] Security scan PASSED — no credential patterns found in logspassed
